pass_summary: All 12 goal truths verified. Wave grouping, parallel execution engine, and cross-platform parity are fully implemented. One minor suggestion about the pre-parallel commit guard being conditionalised on a frontmatter flag rather than applied unconditionally.

issues.title: Pre-parallel commit guard conditionalised on Worktree: Yes flag
issues.description: In targets/claude-code/commands/spec-implement.md Step 2.2c, the pre-parallel commit checkpoint (`git add . && git commit -m 'wip: pre-parallel checkpoint'`) is only triggered when the plan has `Worktree: Yes` in its frontmatter. The plan's Task 2 described this as a general note to always apply before spawning agents with worktree isolation, not as a conditional. Plans executed outside a session worktree (Worktree: No) may still have uncommitted changes that the spawned agents won't see.
issues.suggested_fix: In targets/claude-code/commands/spec-implement.md, change the condition from 'If plan has Worktree: Yes' to 'Commit any pending changes before spawning parallel agents' (unconditional). This ensures agents always fork from a clean, consistent state regardless of the Worktree frontmatter value.
